beats.narration: Visual testing is the oldest and most widely used N D T method — usually first, and often the only check covering the entire part.

payload.title: VISUAL TESTING
payload.sub: The first line of defense in nondestructive inspection.

beats.narration: Every visual test needs three things: a trained eye or camera, enough light on the surface, and access to reach it.

payload.heading: A visual test needs three things

beats.narration: VT reads only the surface — cracks, undercut, porosity, corrosion. Subsurface flaws need radiographic or ultrasonic testing.

payload.heading: VT reads the surface only
payload.note: VT SEES THE SURFACE · BURIED FLAWS → UT / RT

beats.narration: You find the condition, describe it accurately, and record it. The accept-or-reject verdict comes from the governing code — never your opinion alone.
So here's the rule: find it, measure it, record it — then let the criteria decide. That discipline is what makes an inspector worth trusting.

payload.sub: Then let the acceptance criteria decide.
